data.title: Praj Industries Limited Earnings Missed Analyst Estimates: Here's What Analysts Are Forecasting Now
data.description:
Investors in Praj Industries Limited (NSE:PRAJIND) had a good week, as its shares rose 8.0% to close at ₹308 following the release of its quarterly results. Things were not great overall, with a surprise (statutory) loss of ₹0.67 per share on revenues of ₹8.4b, even though the analysts had been expecting a profit. Earnings are an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance, look at what the analysts are forecasting for next year, and see if there's been a change in sentiment towards the company. So we collected the latest post-earnings statutory consensus estimates to see what could be in store for next year.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Following the latest results, Praj Industries' seven analysts are now forecasting revenues of ₹36.5b in 2027. This would be a solid 15% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to soar 284% to ₹10.88. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been anticipated revenues of ₹36.5b and earnings per share (EPS) of ₹11.25 in 2027. The analysts seem to have become a little more negative on the business after the latest results, given the minor downgrade to their earnings per share numbers for next year.

See our latest analysis for Praj Industries

It might be a surprise to learn that the consensus price target was broadly unchanged at ₹376, with the analysts clearly implying that the forecast decline in earnings is not expected to have much of an impact on valuation. The consensus price target is just an average of individual analyst targets, so - it could be handy to see how wide the range of underlying estimates is. Currently, the most bullish analyst values Praj Industries at ₹500 per share, while the most bearish prices it at ₹311. There are definitely some different views on the stock, but the range of estimates is not wide enough as to imply that the situation is unforecastable, in our view.

Looking at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can make sense of these forecasts is to see how they measure up against both past performance and industry growth estimates. We can infer from the latest estimates that forecasts expect a continuation of Praj Industries'historical trends, as the 12% annualised revenue growth to the end of 2027 is roughly in line with the 14% annual growth over the past five years. Compare this with the broader industry, which analyst estimates (in aggregate) suggest will see revenues grow 14% annually. It's clear that while Praj Industries' revenue growth is expected to continue on its current trajectory, it's only expected to grow in line with the industry itself.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that the analysts downgraded their earnings per share estimates, showing that there has been a clear decline in sentiment following these results. Happily, there were no real changes to revenue forecasts, with the business still expected to grow in line with the overall industry. The consensus price target held steady at ₹376, with the latest estimates not enough to have an impact on their price targets.

With that said, the long-term trajectory of the company's earnings is a lot more important than next year. We have forecasts for Praj Industries going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

And what about risks? Every company has them, and we've spotted 2 warning signs for Praj Industries you should know about.

data.title: Roland (TSE:7944) One Off ¥3.8b Loss Tests High Margin Recovery Expectations
data.description:
Advertisement

Roland (TSE:7944) FY 2025 Results Set Up A Margin Story Investors Will Be Watching

Roland (TSE:7944) has just posted its FY 2025 numbers with Q4 revenue of ¥31,382 million and basic EPS of ¥124.06 loss, while the latest trailing twelve months show revenue of ¥100,952 million and basic EPS of ¥81.69. The company has seen revenue move from ¥99,433 million with basic EPS of ¥216.50 on a trailing basis in FY 2024 Q4 to ¥100,952 million with basic EPS of ¥81.69 in FY 2025 Q4, against a backdrop of a one off ¥3.8 billion loss and net profit margins at 2.1% versus 6% a year earlier. Investors are therefore likely to focus on how quickly margins can stabilise from here.

See our full analysis for Roland.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to see how these results line up with the main market narratives around Roland's growth, risks, and profitability, and where the latest margins data pushes back on those storylines.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

¥3.8b One Off Loss Pulls TTM Margin Down To 2.1%

  • On a trailing 12 month view, Roland earned net income of ¥2,168 million on revenue of ¥100,952 million, which works out to a 2.1% net margin compared with 6% a year earlier, with the company highlighting a one off loss of ¥3.8b as a key factor in that gap.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is that the one off ¥3.8b loss is sitting next to otherwise profitable quarters. Q1 to Q3 FY 2025 together show net income of ¥5,438 million on revenue of ¥69,570 million, which heavily supports the bullish angle that underlying operations stayed profitable even though the full year margin reads as only 2.1%.

High 46.9x P/E Versus Peers Despite Margins Squeezing

  • Roland trades on a trailing P/E of 46.9x, compared with a peer average of 10.7x and a JP Leisure industry average of 14.8x, even though trailing net margin for the last 12 months sits at 2.1% versus 6% in the prior year.
  • Critics highlight that a rich multiple on weaker trailing profitability looks like a bearish setup, and the numbers give that some weight, as trailing EPS on a 12 month basis has moved from ¥216.50 to ¥81.69 while the P/E remains 46.9x. However, the presence of a one off ¥3.8b loss means the current P/E partly reflects that temporary hit rather than only the recurring earnings power.
    • Against that, bears also have to acknowledge that net income over the trailing 12 months is still positive at ¥2,168 million, so the high P/E is not being calculated on a loss making base.
    • The tension for a bearish view is that multi year earnings have declined about 4.2% per year, which lines up with the current low 2.1% margin, but the very high P/E shows investors are still willing to pay up despite that history.

Forecast 30.5% EPS Growth Versus 3.4% Revenue Pace

  • Analysts are forecasting earnings to grow about 30.5% per year over the next three years while revenue is expected to grow around 3.4% per year, compared with a JP market revenue forecast of 5% per year and earnings growth of 9.1% per year.
  • Supporters of a bullish case point to this wide gap between earnings and revenue growth, and the current data gives them clear talking points, as Q1 to Q3 FY 2025 combine for positive EPS in each quarter and a trailing EPS of ¥81.69. Yet the market price of ¥3,860 is still about 6.9% below the DCF fair value of ¥4,147.26, which heavily supports the idea that margin repair rather than top line acceleration is central to the bullish story.
    • At the same time, the forecast 30.5% earnings growth is being weighed against the trailing 4.2% annual decline in earnings over the past five years, so the data also reminds bullish investors that the forecast path is quite different to the historical trend.
    • Valuation inputs such as the DCF fair value of ¥4,147.26 versus the current ¥3,860 share price and the high 46.9x P/E make it clear that any future margin improvement will be watched closely alongside these growth expectations.

data.title: Kyowa Kirin (TSE:4151) Valuation Check After Dividend Hike And New Shareholder Return Guidance
data.description:
Kyowa Kirin (TSE:4151) has put shareholder returns in focus after a board meeting to revise its dividend policy, pairing a higher year end dividend with fresh guidance on future payouts and earnings.

See our latest analysis for Kyowa Kirin.

Those dividend and earnings updates arrived alongside a sharp 12.01% 7 day share price return and a 7.99% 90 day share price return, while the 1 year total shareholder return of 33.51% contrasts with slightly negative total shareholder returns over three and five years. Preferred P/E of 21x: Is it justified?

Kyowa Kirin shares last closed at ¥2,691, and on a P/E of 21x the market is asking you to pay more for each yen of earnings than both the broader JP pharmaceuticals industry, at 15.4x, and the peer group, at 19.9x.

The P/E multiple simply compares the share price to earnings per share, so at 21x you are effectively paying 21 years of current earnings upfront. For a pharmaceutical business with established products and a pipeline, P/E often reflects how confident the market is that earnings can be sustained or improved over time.

Here, the picture is mixed. On one side, Kyowa Kirin is flagged as expensive against both the industry and its peer average, which suggests the market is assigning a premium to its earnings. On the other side, the SWS fair P/E estimate sits higher at 23.7x, which points to some scope for the current market multiple to move closer to that fair ratio level if earnings and sentiment stay aligned with that view.

data.title: Coupang Data Breach Lawsuits Test Valuation Upside And Profit Outlook

Coupang, listed on the NYSE under the ticker CPNG, runs a large e commerce and logistics platform in South Korea and has been a key player in online retail in that market. TORIDOLL Holdings (TSE:3397) has put up a mixed Q3 2026 scorecard, with revenue at ¥68.7b and basic EPS of ¥32.84, set against a trailing 12 month picture that shows revenue of ¥276.9b and basic EPS of ¥47.44. Over recent quarters the company has seen revenue move between ¥66.4b in Q4 2025 and ¥71.9b in Q2 2026, while basic EPS has ranged from a loss of ¥51.39 in Q4 2025 to ¥50.15 in Q1 2026, leaving investors to focus on how a 1.5% trailing net margin and the prior ¥7.4b one off loss frame the quality of the current earnings run rate.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

1.5% margin and ¥2.9b Q3 profit show thin profitability

  • Q3 2026 net income was ¥2,883m on revenue of ¥68,692m, which sits inside a trailing 12 month net income of ¥4,158m on ¥276,942m revenue, giving that 1.5% net margin that is lower than last year’s 1.9%.
  • Bulls pointing to the company as a steady earnings compounder need to square that view with the recent profit profile,
    • because the last 12 months include a ¥7.4b one off loss and still only produce ¥4,158m of net income, the earnings base supporting any growth story is relatively slim right now.
    • At the same time, analysts are talking about annual earnings growth of 37.9%, so the current 1.5% margin and modest 12 month profit make it clear that a lot of the bullish case rests on future improvement rather than what has just been reported.

  • Critics who focus on earnings volatility get some support from these swings,
    • because the move from the Q4 2025 loss of ¥51.39 per share to Q1 2026 EPS of ¥50.15 and then down again to ¥15.71 and ¥32.84 shows that per share profitability has not settled into a tight range.
    • When you combine that pattern with the 1.5% trailing net margin, it gives bearish voices clear numbers to point to when they argue that recent profits are still a bit uneven.

    • On the other hand, skeptics highlight that the elevated 89.4x multiple is sitting on top of a trailing net margin of just 1.5%, so the current valuation already bakes in a lot of confidence that those growth forecasts will be delivered.

data.title: Humble Group (OM:HUMBLE) Q3 Loss Tests Bullish Margin Improvement Narratives
data.description:
Humble Group (OM:HUMBLE) has just posted its FY 2025 third quarter numbers with revenue of roughly SEK 2.1b and a basic EPS loss of SEK 0.03, setting the tone for a year where growth expectations and thin profitability sit side by side. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from SEK 1.84b in Q2 2024 to SEK 1.95b in Q3 2024, then to SEK 2.04b in Q4 2024 and SEK 2.10b in Q3 2025. Basic EPS has shifted from SEK 0.07 in Q2 2024 to SEK 0.06 in Q3 2024 and SEK 0.09 in Q4 2024, followed by SEK 0.02, SEK 0.01 and a small loss of SEK 0.03 across the first three quarters of 2025. This leaves investors focused on how modest margins and a reported 0.5% net profit margin on the trailing 12 months frame the latest set of results.

See our full analysis for Humble Group.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely followed growth narrative around Humble and where the story on margins, risks and rewards might need updating.

See what the community is saying about Humble Group

Advertisement

Revenue Near SEK 8.1b, Profit Still Thin

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, Humble booked about SEK 8,080m in revenue and SEK 44m in net income, which works out to a net margin of roughly 0.5% and sits alongside revenue that has been growing at about 6.5% per year versus a Swedish market rate of 0.4% decline per year.
  • Analysts' consensus view points to a business that is growing the top line faster than the broader Swedish market, yet still wrestling with low profitability, as shown by the 0.5% trailing net margin versus 0.9% a year earlier and a one off SEK 18m gain that lifts reported earnings, so this is a growth story that is not yet matched by strong margins.
    • The same consensus expects earnings to grow around 40% per year from today, while historical earnings growth is cited at 51.8% per year over five years, which sits in clear tension with the very slim 0.5% net margin now on the books.
    • Because the SEK 18m one off is embedded in the trailing 12 month figures, that growth profile looks stronger on paper than the underlying 44m net income might suggest, which is why many investors pay close attention to both organic margin trends and any non recurring items.

Curious how this mix of fast revenue growth and thin margins is shaping the story for Humble Group, and how other investors are framing it over time? Before you dig deeper into the numbers you may want to see what the crowd is focusing on in the community narratives for the stock. 📊 Read the full Humble Group Consensus Narrative.

SEK 15m Q3 Loss After Earlier Profits

  • Looking at FY 2025 so far, Humble moved from net income of SEK 11m in Q1 2025 and SEK 5m in Q2 2025 to a net loss of SEK 15m in Q3 2025, with basic EPS going from SEK 0.024631 to SEK 0.011193 and then to a loss of SEK 0.03338, which contrasts with the SEK 42m profit and SEK 0.094049 EPS reported in Q4 2024.
  • For investors who lean on the bullish narrative, this step back into a SEK 15m loss in Q3 2025 tests the idea that operating leverage from new capacity and efficiency measures will steadily improve margins, because the numbers so far show patchy quarterly profitability even as those projects are intended to ramp.
    • The bullish story highlights initiatives like the SEK 80m efficiency program and portfolio streamlining aimed at lifting EBITDA margins, yet the sequential move from a SEK 42m profit in Q4 2024 to SEK 11m, then SEK 5m, then a SEK 15m loss suggests that the benefits of those efforts are not yet clearly reflected in the quarterly net income line.
    • Capacity expansions at Grahns Konfektyr and the additional bar line in Australia are intended to improve fixed cost absorption, but the loss in Q3 2025 serves as a reminder that higher fixed costs can weigh on results in periods where revenue, at SEK 2,095m this quarter, is not enough to cover them comfortably.

Forecasts point to a slower 8.4% earnings growth pace ahead, which can prompt bulls to ask whether the recent strength is already the high point or simply one step in a longer earnings story.

Revenue trends versus earnings trajectory

  • For a bullish narrative that treats Itoki as a workspace solutions provider rather than a simple product seller, the mix of roughly 5.1% revenue growth and faster earnings growth can be read as support for the idea that the business has been finding ways to earn more from each yen of sales.   • Over the last 12 months, Dai Nippon Printing earned net income of ¥80,001 million on ¥1.5b of revenue, which works out to a 5.3% net profit margin compared with 8.9% in the prior year.
  • What stands out for a bearish take is that this thinner 5.3% margin sits alongside modest forecast growth of about 2.35% a year for earnings and 1.4% a year for revenue, which critics highlight as a sign that profitability and growth are both on the low side.

  • Over the last 12 months, Logistea reported a 79.4% net profit margin, compared with 15.2% a year earlier, with a SEK 582 million one off gain playing a big role in that jump.
  • Critics highlight that such a large one off item makes it harder to judge ongoing profitability, even though trailing net income reached SEK 821 million on SEK 1,034 million of revenue.
    • This very high margin is used to support the bullish idea that recent refinancing and cost reductions are flowing through to profit, but the SEK 582 million gain means part of the uplift is not from regular rental activity.

    • On one hand, TTM net income of SEK 821 million versus SEK 332 million in the prior TTM window aligns with the bullish view that refinancing and portfolio growth have lifted profitability.
    • On the other hand, the forecast 0.3% annual earnings decline supports the bearish argument that recent EPS of 0.52 SEK in Q3 2025 and 1.69 SEK over 12 months may not be a straightforward guide for the next few years.
Over the last few quarters Logistea has produced EPS of 0.33 SEK, 0.51 SEK and 0.52 SEK, so bulls argue the recent level of earnings is starting to look more consistent and worth studying in detail before leaning too much on forecasts alone. 🐂 Logistea Bull Case

It's been a good week for SMC Corporation (TSE:6273) shareholders, because the company has just released its latest quarterly results, and the shares gained 9.5% to JP¥70,840. It looks like a credible result overall - although revenues of JP¥210b were in line with what the analysts predicted, SMC surprised by delivering a statutory profit of JP¥671 per share, a notable 19% above expectations. The analysts typically update their forecasts at each earnings report, and we can judge from their estimates whether their view of the company has changed or if there are any new concerns to be aware of. With this in mind, we've gathered the latest statutory forecasts to see what the analysts are expecting for next year.

Taking into account the latest results, the consensus forecast from SMC's 17 analysts is for revenues of JP¥895.4b in 2027. This reflects a notable 10% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to rise 5.6% to JP¥2,636. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of JP¥889.5b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥2,612 in 2027. So it's pretty clear that, although the analysts have updated their estimates, there's been no major change in expectations for the business following the latest results.

The consensus price target rose 8.4% to JP¥66,450despite there being no meaningful change to earnings estimates. It could be that the analystsare reflecting the predictability of SMC's earnings by assigning a price premium. That's not the only conclusion we can draw from this data however, as some investors also like to consider the spread in estimates when evaluating analyst price targets. Currently, the most bullish analyst values SMC at JP¥100,000 per share, while the most bearish prices it at JP¥48,000. This is a fairly broad spread of estimates, suggesting that analysts are forecasting a wide range of possible outcomes for the business.

Of course, another way to look at these forecasts is to place them into context against the industry itself. The analysts are definitely expecting SMC's growth to accelerate, with the forecast 8.2% annualised growth to the end of 2027 ranking favourably alongside historical growth of 5.7% per annum over the past five years. By contrast, our data suggests that other companies (with analyst coverage) in a similar industry are forecast to grow their revenue at 5.1% per year. Factoring in the forecast acceleration in revenue, it's pretty clear that SMC is expected to grow much faster than its industry.

The Bottom Line

The most obvious conclusion is that there's been no major change in the business' prospects in recent times, with the analysts holding their earnings forecasts steady, in line with previous estimates. Fortunately, they also reconfirmed their revenue numbers, suggesting that it's tracking in line with expectations. Additionally, our data suggests that revenue is expected to grow faster than the wider industry. There was also a nice increase in the price target, with the analysts clearly feeling that the intrinsic value of the business is improving.

Following on from that line of thought, we think that the long-term prospects of the business are much more relevant than next year's earnings. We have estimates - from multiple SMC analysts - going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.
